capital.summary: Granules India invested a cumulative ₹23,107 million (approximately $277 million USD) from FY22 to FY26 in the Genome Valley 10 Bn dosages facility, MUPS facility, and a packing facility in the USA.
capital.project_name: Genome Valley 10 Bn dosages facility, MUPS facility, and USA packing facility (Cumulative FY22-FY26)
capital.timeline: Cumulative investment from FY22 to FY26
capital.why_it_matters: These new and expanded pharmaceutical manufacturing and packaging facilities will require a wide range of lab supplies, reagents, consumables, and scientific equipment for R&D, quality control, and production processes.

capital.summary: Granules India invested ₹5,547 million (approximately $66.5 million USD) in FY26 for the Genome Valley 10 Bn dosages facility, MUPS facility, and a packing facility in the USA.
capital.project_name: Genome Valley 10 Bn dosages facility, MUPS facility, and USA packing facility (FY26 portion)
capital.why_it_matters: These new and expanded pharmaceutical manufacturing and packaging facilities will require a wide range of lab supplies, reagents, consumables, and scientific equipment for R&D, quality control, and production processes.

capital.summary: Granules India plans to invest approximately INR 600-odd crores (approximately $72 million USD) in broad-based projects for the upcoming year (FY27), including a new distribution center in the U.S.
capital.project_name: New distribution center in the U.S.
capital.why_it_matters: A new distribution center will require lab supplies and equipment for inventory management, quality checks, and potentially repackaging or kitting operations.

project.summary: Granules India announced an investment of ₹5,547 Mn (approximately $66.5 million USD) in Fiscal Year 2026 for manufacturing facilities, including the Genome Valley 10 Bn dosages facility, a Multi-Unit Pellet System (MUPS) facility, and a packing facility in the USA.
project.scope: Investment in new and existing manufacturing facilities to increase capacity for 10 billion dosages, MUPS production, and packing operations in the USA.
project.why_it_matters: This manufacturing scale-up indicates an increased demand for raw materials, reagents, consumables, and potentially new scientific equipment to support higher production volumes at these named facilities.

regulatory.summary: Granules Pharmaceuticals Inc. has received an updated drug label for Dextroamphetamine Saccharate, Amphetamine Aspartate Monohydrate, Dextroamphetamine Sulfate, and Amphetamine Sulfate Extended-Release Capsules, indicating an Abbreviated New Drug Application (ANDA) marketing status.
regulatory.product_or_site: Dextroamphetamine Saccharate, Amphetamine Aspartate Monohydrate, Dextroamphetamine Sulfate, and Amphetamine Sulfate Extended-Release Capsules
regulatory.why_it_matters: ANDA approval indicates upcoming demand for raw materials, manufacturing consumables, and potentially new equipment for scaled-up production.

ai_summary: Granules Pharmaceuticals is undergoing significant expansion, investing over $200 million in new manufacturing facilities in India and the USA, including a new US distribution center. This massive capital outlay signals increased production capacity and new product launches, like their recently approved extended-release capsule. For lab supply sales, this means substantial opportunities for new equipment, consumables, and services across R&D, QC, and manufacturing operations.
